clinicalProblem.clinicalIntent: Classify BP patterns using office and out-of-office measurements to identify hypertension phenotypes
clinicalProblem.whenToUse: Initial hypertension evaluation, medication decision-making, confirming diagnosis after abnormal office readings
clinicalProblem.decisionOrAction: BP phenotype classification (sustained normotension, sustained hypertension, masked hypertension, white-coat hypertension)

navigationPath.purpose: Entry point for BP phenotype classification
Enter home or ambulatory BP readings
Confirm patient is not taking antihypertensive medication
Apply logic engine to determine BP category
Show next steps based on classification

validationRules: Patient must NOT be taking antihypertensive medication
Office BP should be based on properly measured readings
Out-of-office BP should be based on standardized home or ambulatory monitoring
Both office and out-of-office assessments required for classification

rules: Rule 1: IF officeBP_high == false AND outOfOfficeBP_high == false → Sustained normotension
Rule 2: IF officeBP_high == true AND outOfOfficeBP_high == true → Sustained hypertension
Rule 3: IF officeBP_high == false AND outOfOfficeBP_high == true → Masked hypertension
Rule 4: IF officeBP_high == true AND outOfOfficeBP_high == false → White-coat hypertension
Classification applies only to individuals not taking antihypertensive medication
High BP thresholds should follow current guideline definitions (e.g., ≥130/80 mmHg for out-of-office)

categories.name: Sustained normotension
categories.description: Blood pressure is normal in both clinical and non-clinical settings

categories.name: Sustained hypertension
categories.description: Blood pressure is elevated in both clinical and non-clinical settings

categories.name: Masked hypertension
categories.description: Blood pressure is normal in clinic but elevated out of office (increased cardiovascular risk)

categories.name: White-coat hypertension
categories.description: Blood pressure is elevated in clinic but normal out of office (lower cardiovascular risk than sustained hypertension)

category.description: One of: Sustained normotension, Sustained hypertension, Masked hypertension, White-coat hypertension

thresholds.sbp: N/A for classification (crisis rules apply separately if BP reaches crisis levels)

symptomsToAsk: If BP is severely elevated in either setting, check for hypertensive crisis symptoms

escalationLogic.ifAnySymptomPresent: Treat as hypertensive emergency regardless of classification
escalationLogic.ifNoSymptoms: Proceed with classification and appropriate follow-up

appDisplay.screenType: Standard classification display with risk stratification
appDisplay.behavior: Normal routing unless crisis thresholds met

providerSuggestion.Sustained normotension: Routine follow-up per age/risk factors, repeat screening annually
providerSuggestion.Sustained hypertension: Initiate lifestyle modifications and pharmacotherapy per guidelines
providerSuggestion.Masked hypertension: Confirm with ambulatory monitoring, initiate lifestyle modifications, consider pharmacotherapy if high risk
providerSuggestion.White-coat hypertension: Confirm with repeated out-of-office monitoring, lifestyle modifications, monitor annually for progression to sustained hypertension

followUpTiming.Masked hypertension: 3-6 months for confirmation and treatment initiation

nextStepRouting.repeatMeasurement: Confirm sustained hypertension with repeated out-of-office measurements. White-coat and masked hypertension may require 24-hour ambulatory monitoring for confirmation
nextStepRouting.navigatorEscalation: Flag sustained and masked hypertension for care team review. White-coat hypertension requires monitoring annotation
nextStepRouting.documentation: Log BP category, both office and out-of-office status, and classification date to health record. Flag that patient is not on antihypertensive medication
